div.h3: Syscall Filter Architecture: Trace All, Print Selectively
div.p: Invariant: 100% of syscalls are intercepted — the entry/exit toggle is maintained for every stop. Filtering is output-only, applied at exit stop after timing and stats accumulation.

⚠ WHY Filtering Cannot Skip Interception The toggle is a two-phase protocol — both phases are mandatory to maintain PID state. If the tracer skipped PTRACE_SYSCALL for a "filtered" syscall, the tracee would remain FROZEN at the entry stop forever.
syscall instruction int 0x80 / syscall All 335 syscall numbers
Entry Stop SIGTRAP | 0x80 Tracee frozen before kernel executes syscall orig_rax = syscall nr args in rdi, rsi, rdx
Kernel executes syscall File I/O, network, memory… Return value → rax Duration: 100ns–500ms
Exit Stop SIGTRAP | 0x80 Tracee frozen after kernel returns rax = return value orig_rax still = syscall nr
waitpid(-1, &status, 0) Blocks until ANY traced PID stops Returns PID + packed status word WSTOPSIG = 0x85 (SIGTRAP|0x80)
Entry/Exit Toggle state->in_syscall = !state->in_syscall EVERY stop flips the toggle No exceptions — filter cannot skip without desynchronizing state
Entry Stop Handler in_syscall == 1 extract_args(&regs, &current) clock_gettime(MONOTONIC, &entry_time) Build "[pid N] name(args" No output yet
Exit Stop Handler in_syscall == 0 PTRACE_GETREGS → retval = rax
① clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC) exit_time captured elapsed_ns = diff(entry_time, exit_time)
② stats_record(nr, retval, elapsed_ns) ALWAYS called — no filter check here g_stats[nr].call_count++ g_stats[nr].total_ns += elapsed_ns
③ filter_matches(syscall_nr) Check g_filter names array count == 0 → always true
fputs(state->outbuf, g_output) Single write() syscall Atomic per PID
Discard buffered entry state->outbuf_len = 0 Silently dropped
g_stats[400] call_count, error_count, total_ns Full stats regardless of filter
